name: Suriname

colors.symbolism: the fertile land and abundant forests; hope for the future
the love of country and progress
the golden future, sacrifice, and the nation's bright destiny (the central star)

description: Five horizontal stripes — green (broad), white (thin), red (broadest), white (thin), green (broad) — with a centred yellow five-pointed star on the red stripe.
history: Adopted on 25 November 1975, the day Suriname gained independence from the Netherlands. Designed by Jacques Herman Pinas, the flag was selected from a national competition, with the colours drawn from the principal political parties of the time — green for the agricultural party, red for the progressive party, white for justice and human rights, and yellow for the future.

features.symbolism: green for the country's fertile land and forests, white for justice and freedom, red for the love of country and progress
the unity of the nation's ethnic groups, the spirit of sacrifice, and a hopeful future
